Corporate Mar 30, 2026

Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau to retire by end of Q3 2026

Air Canada announced on March 30, 2026, that President and CEO Michael Rousseau has informed the board of directors of…...

Rousseau's planned exit provides Air Canada's board a 15-month window to identify succession leadership amid ongoing industry consolidation pressures and post-pandemic operational challenges.

See AeroTime Hub →

Corporate Mar 30, 2026

Bombardier extends deadline for bondholders to vote on 2026 debenture changes

Bombardier has pushed back the deadline for bondholders to vote on proposed changes to the terms of its…...

Bombardier's deadline extension signals potential bondholder resistance to debt restructuring terms, suggesting the manufacturer faces pressure managing 2026 maturities amid capital constraints.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Pentagon weighing termination of Raytheon GPS ground control contract after years of delays

Space Force considers shifting to upgraded legacy ground system...

The Space Force's consideration of legacy system alternatives signals potential cost savings and schedule relief, but may create near-term operational gaps during transition.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 30, 2026

U.S. Navy Launches Competition for T-45’s Replacement with Final RFP

The U.S. Navy has released the final Request for Proposals for the Undergraduate Jet Training System which will replace the T-45 Goshawk, confirming an accelerated schedule with a focus on costs. The U.S. Navy has released on Mar. 26, 2026, the final Request for Proposals (RFP) for the...

The Navy's accelerated T-45 replacement timeline signals increased budget pressure on defense contractors to deliver advanced trainer aircraft at competitive prices within compressed development schedules.

See The Aviationist →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Safety Management Systems succeed only when organizational culture encourages blameless incident reporting, enabling proactive hazard identification before accidents occur.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Why Boeing Built The 777X With A New & Elegant Cabin Architecture

Modern, streamlined and elegant, the new cabin architecture could change the passenger experience forever.

Boeing's 777X cabin redesign prioritizes modular flexibility and larger windows to differentiate premium positioning against Airbus A350 in ultra-long-range competition.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Mar 30, 2026

Sonex Announces Immediate Closure

Owner Mark Schaible cites sales decline, lender pressure and cash-flow constraints in video message.

The closure of Sonex signals financial distress in the kit aircraft market, where manufacturers face mounting pressure from rising material costs and shrinking buyer demand amid economic headwinds.

See AVweb →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Spain risks Trump’s wrath as it shuts airspace to US warplanes used in Iran

Spain has closed its airspace to United States (US) military aircraft involved in the war in Iran, according…...

Spain's airspace closure to US military aircraft signals European allies may increasingly restrict support for US Middle East operations, complicating American power projection logistics.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically document why residual risks are acceptable, not just implement controls, creating auditable compliance trails.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Before The F-35 Vs. After: How Air Combat Changed

Examining a paradigm shift in air warfare.

Modern air combat prioritizes beyond-visual-range engagements and sensor fusion over traditional dogfighting, fundamentally reshaping pilot training and aircraft design requirements.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

The race to build orbital data centers is missing its biggest variable: power

Here’s the version of the orbital data center story you keep reading: Elon Musk says space will be the cheapest place to run AI within 36 months. LoneStar announces plans for a lunar data center. NVIDIA’s Vera Rubin Space-1 makes headlines. These are real announcements, and they all have one thing...

Orbital data center viability hinges on solving power generation and distribution in space, a technical constraint that venture announcements consistently underestimate or avoid discussing publicly.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

With Voyager’s help, Icarus Robotics to test free-flyer on ISS

SAN FRANCISCO – Voyager Technologies announced plans March 30 to help Icarus Robotics test a free-flying platform, called Joyride, on the International Space Station. Under the agreement, Voyager will handle payload integration, safety certification, coordination of a 2027 launch, on-orbit...

Commercial space logistics providers are enabling faster ISS payload deployment, with Voyager's integration services potentially reducing time-to-orbit for autonomous robotics platforms.

See SpaceNews →

▶
Safety Mar 30, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure from RNO's dual runways 17L/R requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural complexities that differ from standard departure operations.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Virgin Australia rolls out first-ever retro livery celebrating Virgin Blue era

Virgin Australia has rolled out its first-ever retro livery, painting one of its newest Boeing 737-8 aircraft in…...

Nostalgia marketing through heritage liveries can strengthen brand loyalty by reconnecting customers with airline history while modernizing the fleet.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Trump Says F-35 Sale to Saudi Arabia Has Been Approved

For the second time, President Trump says that the U.S. agreed to sell F-35s to Saudi Arabia as relations between the two countries keep improving. For the second time since late last year, reports of U.S. acquiescence for the sale of F-35A Lightning II stealth fighters to the Kingdom of Saudi...

F-35 sales to Saudi Arabia signal deepening U.S.-Middle East defense partnerships, reshaping regional military balance and opening substantial revenue opportunities for defense contractors.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Delta VIP Travel Perks Suspended For Members Of Congress Amid Budget Dispute

Extremely long wait times to get through security at US airports has been commonplace in recent weeks as a partial government shutdown has forced airport security workers to work without pay until their portion of the budget can be passed by politicians. As a result, Delta Air Lines said on...

Delta's suspension of congressional perks signals airlines are willing to pressure lawmakers on operational issues, potentially reshaping industry-government relations beyond traditional lobbying channels.

See Simple Flying →

▶
General Aviation Mar 30, 2026

KSQL Noise Abatement Departure

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures require pilots to navigate complex terrain while adhering to Class B constraints, demanding precise execution in congested Bay Area airspace.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

14 Hours Nonstop: Inside Virgin Atlantic's New Longest Route

Discover the intriguing backstory behind Virgin's new longest service...

Extended ultra-long-haul operations require significant aircraft modifications and operational restructuring that reshape crew scheduling and passenger service economics.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Five aviation rivalries that define competition in the modern era

Although aviation happens to be one of the most welcoming industries with collaborations, an integral part of what…...

Modern aviation competition balances intense rivalries with essential collaborations, forcing competitors to simultaneously contest and cooperate across supply chains and technology development.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Starcloud achieves unicorn status with $170 million raise for orbital data centers

Starcloud has raised $170 million to accelerate development of its next-generation spacecraft, reaching a $1.1 billion valuation as it awaits permission to deploy an 88,000-strong orbital data center network.

Orbital data centers represent a nascent but well-funded infrastructure opportunity that could reshape satellite operations and ground-based connectivity economics within the next decade.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Mar 30, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airline, airport, and flight levels offers a near-term pathway to accommodate growing demand while reducing environmental impact without waiting for revolutionary technologies.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Best Transpacific Airlines (Premium Comfort Revealed)

For years, flying across the Pacific Ocean meant preparing for one of the most exhausting journeys in commercial aviation. A trip between the US West Coast and East Asia could easily stretch beyond ten hours, and passengers often judged the experience by how well they could simply survive the...

Premium cabin differentiation on transpacific routes has become a competitive battleground where airlines justify higher fares through enhanced comfort rather than speed.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Airbus Bird of Prey drone interceptor fires Frankenburg missile on maiden flight

Airbus Defence and Space has completed the first demonstration flight of its Bird of Prey uncrewed interceptor, with…...

Airbus's Bird of Prey demonstrates autonomous air-to-air combat capability, signaling a shift toward unmanned tactical operations in modern air defense systems.

See AeroTime Hub →

International Mar 30, 2026

ANA Holdings joins Japanese consortium to track CO2 emissions from space

ANA Holdings has joined a Japanese consortium selected to develop satellite and aircraft-based technology for tracking carbon dioxide…...

ANA's participation in a space-based CO2 monitoring consortium positions Japanese carriers to meet increasingly stringent carbon accounting standards before regulatory mandates force compliance.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Finnair to lease Embraer E190-E1 and ATR-72-600 aircraft for Norra operations

A week after announcing its largest order in years, Finnair has signed Letters of Intent (LOI) for the…...

Finnair's leasing strategy for Norra regional operations signals a shift toward flexible capacity management rather than sole reliance on aircraft ownership during its fleet modernization.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Science finds a place on Artemis 2

While the Artemis 2 mission is primarily a test flight, the four astronauts on board will conduct some science during the nearly 10-day mission.

Artemis 2's dual-purpose design enables NASA to validate human spaceflight systems while generating scientific data, maximizing mission efficiency and justifying the extended 10-day duration.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Why Do Airlines Still Invest In Premium Economy Instead Of Business Class?

It seems to be one of the use of space aboard aircraft for maximizing revenue.

Airlines prioritize premium economy expansion over business class because it generates higher revenue-per-square-foot by capturing price-sensitive premium travelers without the cost overhead of lie-flat seats.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

easyJet Flight Attendant Injured As Turbulence Strikes Shortly After Takeoff

An easyJet cabin crew member required emergency medical assistance.

Unexpected turbulence shortly after takeoff highlights the operational challenge of securing cabin crew during initial flight phases when they remain mobile for safety demonstrations.

Military Mar 30, 2026

Saab CEO: Canada could get its first Gripen fighter in five years

Saab’s chief executive said Canada’s existing aerospace infrastructure sets it apart from previous Gripen customers and could support a faster…...

Canada's established aerospace manufacturing base could compress typical Gripen delivery timelines by leveraging domestic supply chains and production capabilities.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Delta A330 Engine Fire Spews Debris Over São Paulo Airport After Takeoff

The situation caused other flights to be diverted due to the subsequent runway closure.

Engine debris from uncontained failures poses cascading operational risks, as runway closures from recovery efforts can ground multiple aircraft beyond the incident aircraft itself.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

7 Reasons Why The F-22 Still Outperforms Newer Jets

While the F-35 now outperforms F-22 as a balanced advanced fighter, the F-22 still wins in its air dominance role and some flying characteristics.

The F-22's sustained superiority in air-to-air combat and maneuverability demonstrates that specialization remains strategically valuable despite the F-35's overall capability advantages.

Confirmed drone incursions into NATO airspace escalate airspace security concerns for European operators and may prompt stricter identification protocols across the region.

Air Cargo Mar 30, 2026

Why Do Cargo Airlines Still Prefer The Boeing 747 Over Newer Jets?

The new generation of jets has pushed out the icons of old, but one is still hanging around and is actually preferred over many of these new options.

The 747's massive cargo volume and established airport infrastructure economics make it more profitable per-ton-mile than newer aircraft despite higher fuel costs, creating unexpected competition for modern freighter conversions.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Indonesia receives final A400M after rerouted delivery flight around Middle East

Indonesia has received its second and final Airbus A400M military transport aircraft, completing a two-aircraft order placed in…...

Indonesia's A400M delivery required Middle East rerouting, highlighting geopolitical constraints now affecting military aircraft ferry routes and operational planning.
